title: Fragrant Uruguayan Cox Apple Stew
description: A garlicky and flavorful stew featuring cox apples, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, suitable for a solo dinner and friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences.
instructions: Preheat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add olive oil and saute the chopped onion until it is translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add sliced cox apples and cook for 2 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add vegetable broth, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Let the stew cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
